description_en: Ayumu (渉), written with 'wade/ford', depicts making one's way across a river. It evokes steady, determined, and thoughtful progress through life's currents.
Ayumu (道), using the kanji for 'path/way' (Michi), carries profound philosophical and martial arts connotations, emphasizing a life of discipline and journey.

meaning_en: A phonetic construction meaning many fine silks.
description_en: Arata (亜羅多) uses very dense, ancient-feeling phonetics. It has an almost Buddhist or highly antique literary atmosphere, sounding very distinguished.
Arata (改) means 'to change/reform'. A sharp, extremely determined single-kanji name representing innovation, self-improvement, and fresh starts.
Arata (革) means 'leather/revolution'. Like 'Kai' (改), it signifies a transformative force, pointing to someone who bravely breaks the mold.
Ikuo (以久男) strings together 'by means of', 'long time', and 'man'. An extremely traditional phonetic style popular softly in the mid-Showa era.

meaning_en: A phonetic construction for a durable husband.
description_en: Ikuo (伊久夫) pairs the soft 'I' (伊) with 'long time' and 'husband'. Evokes a deeply stable, eternally devoted, and classically grounded man.
Ikuo (伊久雄) uses 'hero/male' (雄). Very robust and traditional, indicating a courageous boy set to lead a long, steadfast life.
Isao (以佐男) uses 'help/assistant' (佐) and 'man' (男). Indicates a man whose core nature is deeply reliable, supportive, and grounded.
Isao (伊佐央) ends in 'center' (央). It brings a soft, balanced feeling to the traditionally masculine 'Isao', centering the person in their community.
Isao (伊佐緒) finishes with 'cord/beginning' (緒). Has a slightly softer, almost literary or artistic feel compared to the standard masculine endings.
Isami (伊佐海) pairs the foundational 'I-sa' phonetics with 'sea'. Brings a vast, deeply calm, and endlessly supportive marine feeling.
Isami (伊佐実) utilizes 'truth/fruit' (実). Depicts an extremely grounded, honest person whose efforts invariably bear positive, tangible results.
Isami (伊佐美) ends with 'beauty'. Very delicate and aesthetic, often aiming to capture the beauty of a supportive, graceful spirit.
Isamu (偉) uses the single kanji for 'great/remarkable'. Powerfully majestic, commanding immense respect and carrying huge aspirations.
Isamu (威佐武) uses 'majesty' and 'warrior'. Extremely imposing, carrying the full weight of a highly capable, commanding martial figure.
Isamu (一支武) is an incredibly rare construction using 'one', 'branch/support', and 'warrior'. Feels highly tactical and uniquely historical.
Ichirou (以知朗) uses 'know/wisdom' and 'bright/cheerful'. A deeply intelligent yet undeniably sunny and optimistic variation of the classic first-son name.

meaning_en: A son known for his capabilities.
description_en: Ichirou (以知郎) employs 'know/wisdom' and 'son'. A thoughtful phonetic twist on the ultimate classic name, emphasizing intellect.

meaning_en: A son of a thousand fortunes.
description_en: Ichirou (伊千郎) uses 'thousand' and 'son'. Wishes for immense blessing and longevity, wrapped in incredibly standard, trustworthy phonetics.
Ikki (一旭) pairs 'one' with 'morning sun'. Exceptionally brilliant and striking, visualizing the very first ray of dawn.
Ikki (一希) means 'one hope'. Simple, clear, and powerful. It marks the child as the family's singular, brilliant hope for the future.
Ikki (一旗) means 'one flag'. Evokes the image of a solitary but brave leader raising their standard, full of rallying charisma.
Issei (一斎) uses 'purify/room' (Sai). Carries an exceedingly scholarly, disciplined, and slightly monastic or highly academic air.
Issei (一勢) pairs 'one' with 'force/energy'. Highly dynamic and unstoppable, visualizing a singular, overwhelming wave of momentum.
Issei (一征) implies 'a singular conquest/journey'. Assertive and masculine, marking someone who fearlessly charts a path directly forward.
Ittetsu (一哲) uses 'philosophy/sagacity'. Describes an immensely intelligent, serious thinker who adheres firmly to a singular core ideology.
Ittetsu (一徹) literally means 'stubbornness' or 'to pierce through singularly'. Famously given to men of absolute, unbending conviction and iron will.
Ippei (一兵) points to 'one soldier'. A tough, stoic, completely reliable classic name suggesting courage and steadfast loyalty.
Ippei (一平) matches 'one' with 'peace/flat'. Exceptionally popular historically, promising a smooth, deeply tranquil, and unshakeable life.
Ippei (壱平) uses the complex/anti-forgery kanji for 'one' (壱). Adds a layer of stark formality and gravity to a deeply peaceful name.
Ibuki (伊吹) comes from Mount Ibuki. It literally conjures a 'blowing breeze', offering a very cool, natural, and incredibly refreshing energy.
Ibuki (伊富岐) uses dense phonetics including 'wealth/rich'. It looks very classical and auspicious, heavily grounded in traditional charm.
Ibuki (伊芙樹) uses phonetics involving a type of lotus and tree. Very intricate and leaning towards a lush, vibrant, botanical aesthetic.
Eichi (叡一) combines 'intelligence/sagacity' with 'one/first'. Depicts a brilliant first son characterized by deep, profound foresight.
Eichi (影一) uses 'shadow' and 'one'. Exceedingly cool and slightly edgy, suggesting a sharp, elusive, and powerfully charismatic presence.
Eichi (映一) pairs 'reflect/shine' with 'one'. Highly cinematic and bright, indicating someone whose brilliance naturally reflects upon others.
Eikichi (映吉) couples 'reflect/shine' with the classic 'good luck'. A deeply traditional name wishing for outwardly visible, continuous prosperity.
Eikichi (栄吉) uses 'flourish/glory' and 'luck'. Incredibly auspicious and solid, ensuring the bearer is blessed with lifelong success.
Eikichi (永吉) matches 'eternity' with 'luck'. A very famous, heavily rooted traditional name symbolizing unbroken, everlasting good fortune.
Eigo (叡吾) places 'wisdom' beside the masculine 'I/myself' ('go'). Depicts a self-assured, exceptionally intelligent and insightful young man.
Eigo (栄吾) blends 'flourish/glory' with 'myself'. A strong, deeply anchored name promising a self-made, continuously prosperous life journey.
Eigo (栄悟) finishes with 'enlightenment/understanding'. Suggests a profoundly deep thinker whose wisdom inherently leads to great personal glory.
Eisaku (栄作) merges 'glory' with 'make/build'. Depicts a highly industrious person, actively constructing their own magnificent success.
Eisaku (栄策) uses 'plan/policy' alongside 'glory'. Portrays a sharp-minded planner whose clever strategies guarantee immense social and monetary success.
Eisaku (永作) places 'eternity' with 'make'. Indicates a steady, dependable craftsman or creator whose good works will last for generations.
Eiji (叡司) pairs 'wisdom/sagacity' with 'rule/administer'. Represents a profoundly intelligent individual built perfectly for highly responsible leadership.
Eiji (叡治) utilizes 'wisdom' and 'heal/govern'. Exceedingly reassuring, marking a calm, massively smart person who brings peace to chaos.
Eiji (映司) blends 'reflect/shine' with the solid 'rule' suffix. Signals an active, energetic administrator who shines brightly in social spheres.
Eisuke (映介) combines 'reflect/shine' with 'mediate/help'. Highlights a glowing, intensely supportive personality ready to brilliantly back up others.
